items.summary: Our deception technology is able to reroute attackers into honeypots, where they believe that they found their real target. The attacks brute forced passwords for RDP credentials to connect to the victim download and execute a previously undetected malware, which we named Trojan.sysscan.

items.title: The Nansh0u Campaign – Hackers Arsenal Grows Stronger
items.summary: In the beginning of April, three attacks detected in the Guardicore Global Sensor Network (GGSN) caught our attention. All three had source IP addresses originating in South-Africa and hosted by VolumeDrive ISP (see IoCs).

items.title: PLEASE_READ_ME: The Opportunistic Ransomware Devastating MySQL Servers
items.summary: Guardicore Labs uncovers a Ransomware detection campaign targeting MySQL servers. Attackers use Double Extortion and publish data to pressure victims.

items.title: New ClamAV security patch closes seven scanner bugs dating back two decades
items.summary: Open source antivirus scanning sits inside mail gateways, file upload checks, and endpoint tooling at organizations of every size. Much of that work runs through ClamAV, the scanning engine maintained by Cisco’s Talos group. The project released two patch versions, 1.5.3 and 1.4.5, carrying fixes for seven security flaws along with smaller hardening changes. Packer and PE parsing flaws Most of the patched bugs sit in the code that unpacks and parses executable formats, the …  More  &

items.title: Week in review: SimpleHelp vulnerability exploited, Oracle EBS Payments flaw under attack
items.summary:
Here’s an overview of some of last week’s most interesting news, articles, interviews and videos: Companies keep bolting AI onto their products, and the security bill is coming due Companies keep bolting AI and LLM features onto their products, and the security results are starting to show a pattern. The vulnerabilities those features create get rated high risk far more often than anything else, and they get fixed slower than anything else. The figures come …  More  →

items.summary:
Posted by Eli Schwartz on Jul 05 OpenClaw is specifically designed such that, unlike, other mechanisms of 
operating an LLM, no human is involved at *any* stage whatsoever. Not 
even to produce a prompt instructing the LLM to work on particular things. 
 
If a human wanted to be involved with the LLM operation, e.g. by 
reviewing things, then the human simply would not use OpenClaw. I very 
much doubt that a human would prompt OpenClaw to ask for human review, 
and if a human *did* do...

items.title: Pogačar hits back at rival as teammate wins stage two of Tour de France
items.summary: Tadej Pogačar and his UAE Team Emirates-XRG responded to Visma Lease a Bike's stage 1 victory with a superb one-two on stage 2 of the Tour de France.

items.title: Council demands illegally cleared Sydney land be restored despite secret sale
items.summary: In 2020, Amir Abu Abara illegally cleared land in Barden Ridge to build a lavish mansion. After a fine, a replanting works order, and the rejection of his housing plan, he is no longer the site's owner.

items.title: Football tragedy puts spotlight on cricket pitches on ovals
items.summary: Nathan Fitzgerald's club has asked authorities to look at the way hard cricket pitches are covered on multi-use ovals as the 27-year-old continues to receive end-of-life care.

items.title: Djokovic overcomes 'meltdowns' to topple Federer's Wimbledon record
items.summary: Novak Djokovic fights off a plucky opponent and his own demons to etch his name into another corner of the record books at Wimbledon.

items.title: Cannibalism probe in 4yo's death on NSW Central Coast
items.summary: Police sources confirm to the ABC that cannibalism will form part of an investigation into the death of a four-year-old boy on the New South Wales Central Coast.

items.title: Declining vaccination rates behind whooping cough spike across WA's south
items.summary: Whooping cough cases across WA's Great Southern have already more than doubled since last year, with doctors expecting the figure to grow further.

items.title: Golden flower cutting the cost of urea fertiliser for farmers
items.summary: A colourful legume crop is emerging as a solution to the rising cost of fertiliser, significantly reducing the need for urea.

items.title: PM apologises for saying he would 'shag' Kylie Minogue in podcast game
items.summary: Anthony Albanese made the comments during a game of "shag, marry, date" on the Bush Deep podcast on Friday, which have been widely condemned as inappropriate.

items.title: Man charged over Brisbane park death
items.summary: The 27-year-old Yeronga man allegedly struck a 66-year-old man, causing him to fall and seriously injure his head.

items.title: Regional state high school parents seek help with teacher shortage
items.summary: Parents are removing their children from state schools after subjects are dropped from senior curricula.

items.title: Bomb the Arctic, dam the Mediterranean and build a second moon: five outlandish plans to remodel our climate
items.summary: Humans have long sought to geoengineer the Earth’s environment.  Tim Flannery  outlines a few of the wildest ideas from the 20th century  An increasing number of scientists think we have let the climate crisis fester for so long that our only hope to stave off ever-intensifying catastrophes is to use technological interventions. Cloud brightening, injecting sulphur into the atmosphere and the use of tiny mirrors in space – all of which might reduce the amount of sunlight reaching Earth’s surface

items.title: Maria Takolander’s futuristic novel of ‘everyday horrors’ follows a mother’s quest to save her son
items.summary: The End of Romance is a relatable dystopia that amplifies humanity’s burning problems – but offers a glint of hope

items.title: Upside-down whales aren’t sick or hurt – they’re just resting
items.summary: This behaviour is unique, rather perplexing and not reported in other large whales.

items.title: We assume students see pictures in their minds as they learn. But not everyone can
items.summary: Every day in class, students are encouraged to ‘visualise’, ‘imagine’ or ‘picture’ concepts in their minds. Some find this impossible.

items.title: Trump's patriotic speech, colossal fireworks and white nationalists: US marks 250 years
items.summary: Donald Trump has commemorated the 250th anniversary of America's independence after storms prompted a roughly two-hour evacuation of the National Mall.

items.title: On fourth of July, an American pope sends a symbolic letter to the US about immigrants
items.summary: The American pope spent 4 July praying for migrants at the epicentre of Europe's migration debate.

items.title: Welcome to the global Las Vegas, where every sport’s a casino and every watcher’s a punter
items.summary:
As the World Cup has shown, sport has exploded as a globalised betting industry. Just look at our very own multibillion-dollar deal between the NRL, Nine and DAZN’s Foxtel. 
 The post  Welcome to the global Las Vegas, where every sport’s a casino and every watcher’s a punter  appeared first on  Crikey .

items.title: The man no-one wants to face - Haaland fires Norway to history as they knock Brazil out
items.summary: Erling Haaland has scored seven goals in four World Cup games and he is the striker that nobody wants to face as he fires Norway into an historic quarter-final.

items.title: Former partner of journalist Lyra McKee hopes her death haunts killer
items.summary: Sara Canning described the gunman who killed Lyra as "pathetic" and believes there will never be justice.

items.title: Police leadership needs fundamental overhaul, review finds
items.summary: Development and recruitment processes in the service need reforming, an independent report says.

items.summary: Rate the Mexico and England players out of 10 below and come back 30 minutes after full-time to see the final ratings.

items.title: Red card system in disarray over Trump, Fifa and Balogun decision
items.summary: The decision to effectively overturn the red card shown to United States forward Folarin Balogun leaves many unanswered questions.

items.title: The Papers: 'Farage: I've done nothing wrong' and 'hangover of God'
items.summary: Reports Reform UK leader Nigel Farage did not declare benefits from an ally in the year before he became an MP lead a number of papers.

items.title: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
items.summary: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

items.title: Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall on US Pacific islands with huge wind gusts
items.summary: The storm, with winds of nearly 290km/h (180mph) and gusts of 350km/h, is lashing the island of Rota.

items.summary: Erling Haaland has scored seven goals in four World Cup games and he is the striker that nobody wants to face as he fires Norway into an historic quarter-final.

items.title: Belgium ‘astonished’ at FIFA’s U-turn on Balogun red card for USA match
items.summary: Belgium's football federation is 'investigating all potential options' to safeguard football after FIFA's U-turn.

items.title: How a son rescued his father from the rubble of Venezuela’s earthquakes
items.summary: After the June 24 earthquakes, ex-firefighter Jesus Garcia found himself working to save his dad and younger brothers.

items.title: Palestinian baby dies in West Bank after Israel blocks urgent medical care
items.summary: Israeli forces also shoot dead a 16-year-old boy in the occupied West Bank and kill two Palestinians in Gaza.

items.title: Turkiye gears up for its first NATO summit in 22 years
items.summary: Leaders of the NATO member states are expected in Turkiye by July 7 for a summit - the first it will host in 22 years.

items.title: Mexico vs England LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026 – last 16
items.summary: Live coverage and text updates from the delayed start ahead from our commentary stream of Mexico vs England.

items.title: 10,000 people evacuated as wildfire tears through southern France
items.summary: A fast-moving wildfire has forced around 10,000 people to evacuate after burning more than 1,500 hectares in France.

items.title: 8 injured, including 4 children, in New York City Fourth of July shooting
items.summary: A shooting at a Fourth of July celebration in the US city of New York wounded eight victims, four of whom are children.

items.title: 12-tonne whale removed from DR Congo beach
items.summary: A dead whale weighing about 12 tonnes has been recovered from the beaches of Muanda.

items.title: How Meta’s Threads Became as Popular as X
items.summary: The social platform that Meta once positioned as a rival to Elon Musk’s X now has 500 million monthly users. It increasingly resembles Reddit.

items.title: Europol's Cyber Defenders wins 2026 European Ombudsman Award for Good Administration
items.summary: Learning through playCyber Defenders is a free-to-play game built on Roblox, one of the platforms most popular with children today. Rather than lecturing young players about online dangers, the game places them inside interactive missions where they learn, through play, how to spot and respond to threats such as fraud, identity theft, and grooming.The initiative was developed by drawing on...

items.title: 156 individuals identified in first-of-its-kind drug-facilitated sexual assaults operation
items.summary: From 22 to 24 June 2026, 29 investigators gathered at the National Crime Agency (NCA) headquarters in London. There, they collaborated closely to identify perpetrators, victims, misogynist groups, and new investigative leads as part of Project Medusa.Launched in April 2026, Project Medusa is a collaborative effort aiming to disrupt and dismantle online networks that facilitate DFSA, while strengthening cross-border strategies...

items.title: Criminal opportunism: how the EU’s most threatening criminal networks exploit society
items.summary: Today, Europol published its latest report ‘Decoding the EU’s most threatening criminal networks: Issue 2 – The blueprint of criminal opportunism’, offering an in-depth analysis of the evolving threat posed by organised crime in the European Union. The report reveals how criminal networks continuously adapt, regenerate, and exploit opportunities despite sustained law enforcement pressure.

items.title: Drug traffickers brought down after shipping 1.4 tonnes of cannabis by parcel post
items.summary: Criminals exploited parcel delivery servicesThe investigation uncovered an organised crime group that had exploited commercial parcel delivery networks to transport cannabis from the Barcelona area to the Rennes region of France. The network is believed to have been led by three key suspects: two based in Spain who coordinated the supply and shipment of the drugs, and one based in...

items.title: Global cyber strike disrupts SocGholish, Amadey, and StealC malware networks
items.summary: Europol together with partners from across the globe today announces a landmark blow to cybercriminal networks as part of Operation Endgame, a sweeping international operation targeting the criminal infrastructure behind ransomware and malware like SocGholish, Amadey, and StealC. In coordinated actions over the past two weeks, key components of these malicious toolkits were dismantled as part of a public-private effort.

items.title: Press conference: new edition of the Europol mapping report
items.summary: On 26 June 2026 at 10:00, a press conference will be held to present the key findings of the report "Decoding the EU’s most threatening criminal networks: Issue 2 – The blueprint of criminal opportunism." This event will be organised jointly by the Cyprus Presidency of the Council of the EU, the European Commission, and Europol.During the press conference, speakers...

items.title: Europol and INTERPOL strengthen partnership against global criminal threats
items.summary: Europol and INTERPOL have renewed their joint commitment to combatting transnational organised crime, agreeing on a new set of Joint Key Operational Priorities (JKOP) that will guide cooperation between the two organisations in the year ahead.

items.title: Counter-terrorism operation disrupted Islamic State Khorasan Province activities
items.summary: Europol, in coordination with eight European law enforcement agencies and the United States Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), successfully concluded a week-long operational action targeting the so-called Islamic State Khorasan Province (ISKP) and its support networks across Europe. The ISKP remains a significant threat in jihadi terrorism, with its networks in Europe posing a major concern.

items.title: Ten arrested in crackdown on counterfeit firearms trafficking route from Türkiye to the EU
items.summary: French authorities, supported by Europol and the Swiss Federal Police (Fedpol), have dismantled a criminal network trafficking counterfeit and converted firearms from Türkiye into the European Union. During coordinated action days between 8 and 11 June 2026, investigators arrested ten suspects and raided seven luxury properties in southern France.

items.title: From The Hague to the host cities: Europol supports FIFA World Cup 2026
items.summary: Europol is supporting the security efforts surrounding the FIFA World Cup 2026, the largest football tournament in history. Hosted across three countries and 16 host cities, the competition will bring together 48 national teams competing in 104 matches across the United States, Mexico and Canada.
